prompt: Create a simple 3D scene using Three.js. Place a colorful rotating cube at the center of the screen that spins slowly. Set the background to black, with each face of the cube displaying a different color (red, blue, green, yellow, purple, orange). Add a point light shining from the upper right to create light and shadow on the cube's surfaces. Use the Three.js CDN.

test_cases.name: Canvas渲染
Three.js scene keeps a centered cube on a black background instead of any generic canvas shell

steps.expression: (()=>{const bg=getComputedStyle(document.body).backgroundColor||'';const nums=(bg.match(/\d+/g)||[]).map(Number);const dark=nums.length<3||(nums[0]+nums[1]+nums[2])<80;return dark&&document.querySelectorAll('canvas').length>=1;})()

test_cases.name: Three.js scene keeps a live WebGL canvas surface with nontrivial geometry

prompt: Create a Three.js space scene. Center: Detailed space station model (multiple cylindrical modules + rectangular solar panels + connecting pipes) rotating slowly. Background: Starfield (500 random small white spheres), Earth (large blue sphere below, with simple atmospheric glow effect simulated using Glow shader). Sun in the distance (strong directional light). Small spacecraft flying in from afar approaching the space station (animated path). Particle effects: Particle jets from space station exhaust ports. Camera: Cinematic slow push-in. Post-processing: Simple glow effect (simulated using additional canvas overlay).

test_cases.name: Canvas renders particle system

prompt: Create an audio visualization particle effect using Three.js (simulated, no real audio required). Arrange 2000 particles in a ring formation. Use sine waves to simulate audio data driving the particles' Y-axis displacement, creating a wave undulation effect. Particle colors change based on Y displacement (low=blue, mid=green, high=red). The entire ring rotates slowly. Mouse controls the camera/view. Frequency and amplitude adjustable via sliders. Use Three.js CDN.

prompt: Create a Three.js Galaxy particle generator. Simulate a spiral galaxy with 10,000 particles. Adjustable parameters (slider UI): number of spiral arms (2-6), rotation angle, random dispersion, particle size, inner/outer radius. Particle colors gradient from center (white hot) to edge (blue cold). Bright core at the center (large glowing sphere). Dust lane effects (some dark semi-transparent large particles). The entire galaxy rotates slowly. Mouse OrbitControls. Performance display (FPS counter). Use Three.js CDN + custom Shader (vertex/fragment shader to make particles attenuate brightness based on distance).
